rag_rules: Use AGREE syntax: assume "label": expression;, guarantee "label": expression;, eq name : type = expression;, const name : type = value;, and assign output = expression;
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes when referring to component ports
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, e.g., 0.0 -> pre(t) + step
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators: and, or, not(...), and implication =>
generic_rules: Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees only when concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations are available
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in contracts
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed through ports or constants
Use valid AGREE scalar types: int, real, and bool
Use exact visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not invent declarations
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence
Branching logic should use nested if ... then ... else
Use AGREE logical operators for contracts
anti_patterns: Do not invent declarations for unresolved identifiers
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar types
